methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: ElevenLabs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Pre-IPO), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
Waymo, a pre-IPO artificial intelligence and software company, is valued at $126B following $16B+ in total fundraising. The peer universe comprises 8 same-sector comparables across similar valuation tiers, including 1 public company (Palantir at $400B) and 7 private firms (OpenAI at $300B, xAI at $250B, Anthropic at $183B, and Databricks at $134B among the sample).

Peer-median analysis, the primary applicable valuation method, identifies a median valuation of $158.5B across the 8-company comp set, with a range spanning $29.3B to $400B. This method directly reflects current market pricing for AI & Software businesses at comparable scale and development stage. The peer cohort's wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in technology focus, commercialization maturity, and investor sentiment within the frontier AI sector.

The triangulated valuation range supports $134.7B (low), $158.5B (median), and $182.3B (high), implying upside of 7–45% from the current $126B valuation. Key uncertainties include the timing and revenue trajectory of autonomous driving commercialization, relative valuation multiples as the AI sector matures, and the extent to which pre-IPO private valuations reflect systematic risk premiums versus public-market equivalents.
